title: gypsocallis longipedunculata
side_effects: Gypsocallis longipedunculata can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting large quantities of the plant's leaves or flowers can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap may lead to skin rashes, eczema, and even burns, especially in people with sensitive skin. Improper use of Gypsocallis longipedunculata may also trigger asthma attacks and other respiratory issues in susceptible individuals.
